---
title: "Chapter 5 — Progress Timeline"
subtitle: "Ch 5 · living document of progress"
author: Cali Pfleger
date: last-modified
bibliography: ../literature/bibliography.bib
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    footer: "Ch 5 progress · auto-updated nightly · calipfleger@ucsb.edu"
    fig-align: center
    self-contained: false
---

## How this deck works {.smaller}

This is a **living timeline** of progress on Chapter 1.

- The nightly pipeline appends a date-stamped section after midnight every day.
- Each daily section includes a short summary of what was worked on plus any new figures.
- Newest entries appear at the **bottom** — navigate forward (→) through time, backward (←) for older.

For the curated talk version, see [chapter5-slides.html](chapter1-slides.html).

## Status legend

- ✅ figure finalized for chapter
- 🔄 figure under iteration
- ⚠ result preliminary, do not cite

. . .

The "What we did" lines are pulled from the daily-summaries the nightly loop writes; figures are mirrored from `chapter-5-climate-tech-sentiment/figures/`.

# Timeline {.center}

_New entries auto-appended below this divider every night._

<!-- PROGRESS-INSERT-MARKER — phase 5 of the nightly pipeline appends new sections HERE -->

---

## 2026-04-23 — Nightly pipeline {.smaller}

**Literature:** Phase-4 AUTO block appended to `chapter5.qmd` and `literature-review.md` on climate discourse framing.

- Liu et al. (2025): diachronic *China Daily* vs. *NYT* framing comparison — outlet-level framing differences may confound VADER topic-level scores. ⚠ relevance uncertain — Cali to verify.
- Freeman (2025): political ecology critique of NbS framing — academic debates may precede/shape media narratives Ch5 tracks.

**Bib:** 2 new entries (`liu2025discourse`, `freeman2025nbs`). All 16 citations resolve ✓.

## References {.scrollable}

::: {#refs}
:::
